name: send-to-busy-pane
query: My codex pane is mid-turn working on something, but I need it to stop and run the tests now. How do I do that from Telegram?
expected_behavior: Chooses /send! (alias /interrupt or /isend) issued inside that pane's forum topic to interrupt the running turn and then deliver the instruction.

Explains the busy-vs-idle distinction: /send queues for the turn boundary on a busy agent (never lost), while /send! interrupts the running turn (Esc, wait for idle) and then delivers; an agent runs one turn at a time so you can only queue or interrupt, never inject mid-turn.

Notes the multi-pane fail-closed caveat: in per-space mode a top-level message only routes when the topic has exactly one live pane, otherwise herdres fails closed and you must reply inside the specific pane thread or use /send.
